notes: The canonical operation kinds are insert, replace, and delete.
No remove operation kind or alias is part of the shared contract.
CRISPR owner and payload boundaries can be carried as first-class request descriptors.
Callable destination semantics can be transported as data, but recipe execution still needs a portable step envelope.
Provider-neutral replace, insert, delete, and ambiguity fail-closed semantics are represented as execution reports.

required_primitives.name: content_recipe_envelope
required_primitives.native_contract: Represent template content, destination content, ordered steps, runtime context, final content, diagnostics, and step reports without file-system orchestration.
notes: This is the immediate bridge between fixture-only CRISPR acceptance and executable templating recipes.

required_primitives.name: recipe_step_envelope
required_primitives.native_contract: Represent ordered smart_merge, partial_merge, and structured_edit steps as declarative data with deterministic per-step reports.
notes: The shared step kinds should be native primitives, not arbitrary Ruby script execution.

required_primitives.name: heading_section_selector
required_primitives.native_contract: Select a heading-owned section branch by normalized heading text, level constraints, and same-or-shallower boundary semantics.
notes: This is the best first real-world acceptance target because README preservation is structurally generic.

required_primitives.name: comment_region_owned_selector
required_primitives.native_contract: Select a structural owner plus leading comment region marker, with explicit match limits and trailing-gap ownership.
notes: This covers Rakefile scaffold cleanup and other managed Ruby source chunks.

required_primitives.name: native_replace_recipe_step
required_primitives.native_contract: Execute a replace request inside a recipe step, fail closed on cardinality mismatch, and report captured text, changed status, and diagnostics.
notes: This should reuse the shared structured-edit request/result/application shape.

required_primitives.name: native_insert_recipe_step
required_primitives.native_contract: Execute an insert request inside a recipe step, including explicit destination anchors and if_missing append behavior.
notes: This is needed for template-managed snippets that must be inserted near an anchor.

required_primitives.name: native_delete_recipe_step
required_primitives.native_contract: Execute a delete request inside a recipe step using canonical operation_kind delete.
notes: Conversational remove maps to delete for discussion only and must not be encoded as an alias.

required_primitives.name: declarative_signature_matching
required_primitives.native_contract: Represent node signatures for matching declarations without executable language-specific callbacks.
notes: Gemfile and gemspec templating need match profiles for source, ruby, git_source, gem, group, eval_gemfile, assignments, and dependencies.

required_primitives.name: named_native_policy
required_primitives.native_contract: Represent domain policies such as preserve README sections, preserve semantic H1, canonicalize Unreleased changelog headings, and rewrite gemspec fields as named portable policies with diagnostics.
notes: Policies may be implemented natively per language, but their request and report shape must remain shared.

script_classifications.shared_contract: Translate each behavior into a declarative primitive or named native policy.
notes: Do not add arbitrary Ruby, JavaScript, Go, or Rust script execution to the shared recipe contract.
Uses heading-section selectors and replace steps.
This is a narrow heading-line policy because an H1 section may span the whole document.
Uses structural selectors, fuzzy/satellite match metadata, and canonical delete.
Should become portable policy diagnostics rather than a Ruby callback.
Requires heading-section replacement plus ordered list-item preservation.

non_goals: Do not port kettle-jem's RubyGem scaffold orchestration as part of ast-merge or ast-crispr.
Do not require Prism or Markly projection APIs in Go, Rust, or TypeScript.
Do not encode remove as an operation kind or alias.
Do not add arbitrary executable script callbacks to the shared recipe contract.
Do not include token resolution in the AST merge substrate; assume template content is already resolved.
